content:
https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/mobile-email-setup

According to the spectrum website the following email address ending: @social.rr.com should be using the twc server settings:

IMAP

  • Incoming Email Server: mail.twc.com
  • Port: 993
  • Connection Security: SSL/TLS
  • Authentication Method: Normal Password
  • User name : full email address

SMTP

  • Outgoing Email Server: mail.twc.com
  • Port: 587
  • Connection Security: STARTTLS
  • Authentication Method: Normal Password
  • User name : full email address

The password you would use is the same one you use to access the webmail account via a browser. It is case sensitive so be careful.

I did locate a question regarding spectrum and passwords dated last year. It's worth reading regarding a password issue: https://support.mozilla.org/mk/questions/1488695
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
re : I've also tried using my Thunderbird user name and Password instead of my full name and Spectrum password with the same results. I know my Thunderbird user name is correct but I can't be 100% sure that my Thunderbird password is correct.

There is no such thing as a Thunderbird username nor password. Thunderbird does not offer a server nor email addresses to general public.

There is a Mozilla username and password which you would use to access and post in this Mozilla Support Forum via a browser, but that has nothing to do 'Thunderbird' email client.

If you have an account on Spectrum server then you need to use the password that gets you access to that webmail account on server - Thunderbird would need to use the same password to access that server.
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
Ako prvé musíte povoliť IMAP v Gmaile: Pred pridaním účtu sa uistite, že IMAP sa zapne otvorením Gmail vo vašom prehliadači a prejdete na Nastavenia> Presmerovanie a POP / IMAPa výber Povoliť IMAP, Pridať do Thunderbird: Choďte Nastavenia účtu > Akcie účtu > Pridať účet pošty (alebo kliknite na tri riadky v pravom hornom rohu). Autentifikácia: Ak sa zobrazí výzva, prihláste sa cez bezpečné okno prehliadača Google a kliknite na Povoliť

Thunderbird zvyčajne automaticky detekuje nastavenia Gmail IMAP, ktoré vyžadujú iba váš e-mail a heslo, Ak ho však musíte nastaviť manuálne alebo overiť svoju konfiguráciu, použite tieto presné podrobnosti servera:

Prichádzajúce nastavenia pošty (IMAP) Server: imap.gmail.com Port: 993 Zabezpečenie pripojenia: SSL/TLS Metóda overovania: OAuth2 Používateľské meno: Vaša úplná adresa Gmail (napr. name@gmail.com)

Nastavenia odchádzajúcej pošty (SMTP) Server: smtp.gmail.com Port: 465 alebo 587 Zabezpečenie pripojenia: SSL/TLS (ak používate port 465) alebo STARTTLS (ak používate port 587) Metóda overovania: OAuth2 Používateľské meno: Vaša úplná adresa Gmail

content:
Try this.

Go to TB menu > Settings > General

- scroll all the way down and click the 'Config editor' button on the right

- click 'Accept the risk and continue' if that appears

- search for network.protocol-handler.warn-external

- change network.protocol-handler.warn-external.http and network.protocol-handler.warn-external.https to true

---

Click a docx link in a message a couple of times. The first time, nothing should happen. I'm not sure what might happen the second time.

---

Close the config editor tab, then

- go to TB menu > Settings > General > Files & Attachments

- there may be a new entry for docx

- click the drop-down menu and choose docx

- if it doesn't appear, click 'Use other...' > navigate to the app's installation folder > choose the app's.exe file

- go back to the config editor tab and and reset the two options

content:
Ed, thank you so much for your suggestions. I have marked this item as providing the solution, but am posting this additional detail which may indicate that it might be a solution to my use case, but not others precisely.

Having a quarter of a century and several gigabytes of history in the wonderful Thunderbird product, I am still cautiously sitting at version 140 and some of the reasons I was getting this failure may derive from that fact. For example, just following your nice instructions to get to Advanced Preferences nearly failed because in this older version the the layout handler does not properly squeeze the Config.Editor button into view from its right-alignment setting. And even after getting over those hurdles, in what is not working the Files&Attachment options that we coerced to appear are very different with regards to the different file type suffixes appropriate to word Documents. This I discovered by forwarding the failing email with the .docx attachments to my laptop which is running Thunderbird at version 150 where clicking on said attachments behaved properly.

So, again, thank you so much for a timely and helpful assistance and for any others hitting a similar symptom I think the message may be that the fix is likely version dependent.

content:
Orddu said
Is there any way to make that custom list a default for all reminders? Or at least some sort of template?

Is the following what you want?

(A) To set default reminders in the global level:

"Settings" > "Calendar" > "Notifications" section > "Show notifications for upcoming events" > Click "+ Add notification" button.

(B) To set default reminders in the calendar level:

1. In Calendar mode, under "Calendars" section, right-click the calendar which you want to configure.

2. In the pop-up context menu, click "Properties".

3. In the "Edit Calendar" dialog: "Notifications" section > "Show notifications for upcoming events" > Click "+ Add notification" button.

Please see the attached images.

content:
Orddu said
That's popup toasts in the notification area though isn't it, which show for 5 seconds and that's it. If you're not glued to your screen at the time, you'll miss them.

If you are using Windows 10 or Windows 11, the following link might be helpful: Notifications won't stay https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1499366

The above link talks about notifications of email arrival, but it works for notifications of event reminders, too.

In "Settings" of Windows 10, you need to enable "Show notifications in action center" so that you can find notifications in action center.

In "Settings" of Windows 11, you need to enable "Show notifications in notification center" so that you can find notifications in notification center.
